title: malus coronaria var. ioensis
intro: Malus coronaria var. ioensis, also known as the Ohio apple or Appalachian apple, is a rare and endangered variety of wild apple tree native to the eastern United States. The medicinal properties of Malus coronaria var. ioensis include its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Malus coronaria var. ioensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although its availability is highly limited due to its endangered status.
